author: Sofia Martínez
title: Local SEO in the AI Era: The 5 Things That Still Work (and 3 That Don't)
description: AI search has reshuffled local rankings. Some tactics that drove traffic in 2023 are now dead weight. Here's what actually moves the needle for local businesses in 2026.

sections.p: Local SEO looked simpler in 2022: get on Google Maps, collect reviews, build some citations, rank for '[service] near me.' AI Overviews, ChatGPT Search, and Perplexity have added complexity — but they haven't broken local SEO. They've just raised the floor on what 'good enough' looks like.

list: Google Business Profile optimization — still the single highest-leverage action for any local business. Updated hours, services, photos, and a consistent review response cadence all directly affect Maps ranking.
Local landing pages — city-specific pages with real local content (not just 'serving [City] since 2015') continue to rank well for geo-modified queries.
Review velocity — not just count. Consistent new reviews outperform a one-time burst. Monthly review requests outperform nothing.
NAP consistency — name, address, and phone matching across all directories remains a trust signal that AI engines use to validate business information.
Local schema markup — LocalBusiness, Service, and FAQPage schema directly feeds AI answer engines and improves rich result eligibility.

sections.p: Keyword stuffing in GBP descriptions — Google now penalizes unnatural keyword density in Business Profile fields. Thin service pages with one paragraph per service — AI engines skip them; they need real depth to cite you. Purchased citations from low-authority directories — the signal value has dropped to near zero and some now actively hurt trust scores.

The businesses dominating local search in 2026 are publishing consistently. Not viral content — useful, local, specific content. A plumber who publishes 'common water heater problems in [city] and what they cost to fix' owns that query. The bar is low because most local businesses still aren't publishing at all.

sections.h2: The review response pattern that builds ranking

sections.p: Responding to every review — positive and negative — within 24 hours with a personalized (not templated) response is now a measurable ranking signal. Google's AI can detect template responses and discounts them. The businesses consistently responding with genuine, specific replies rank higher and convert better on Maps.

sections.quote: Local SEO in 2026 rewards the same thing it always did: being genuinely useful to the people in your area. AI just raises the bar on what 'useful' looks like.
